fields.description:
# Finish Jira hard-delete -> outbound re-create (replaces lost epic-3e36)

## Why
On a Jira hard-delete the reconciler preserves local content (per ADR 0027/0028) BUT never re-creates the Jira
issue — the `follow_on` re-create payload is built and never consumed, and the branch writes a COMMENT falsely
claiming "outbound re-create follow-on emitted". Unintentional gap (docstring/TODO state re-create as intended;
no ADR recorded dropping it; tracking epic `3e36` lost at store re-creation). Finish the feature.

Citations: `apply_inbound.py:541-560` (hard_delete branch, TODO(epic-3e36) at :560); `applier.py:469-471`
(follow_on consumer only handles `kind=="suppress_pair"`); `reconcile.py:68-79` (no dispatch entry);
`.joe-janitor/report-2026-07-02.md:137`.

## What (corrected routing + wiring)
1. Key mismatch: `_apply_inbound_delete` branches on `payload.get("probe_outcome")` but the prod probe router
   emits `reason`. CANONICAL = `reason` (already emitted by prod): update `_apply_inbound_delete` to read
   `reason`; update the existing unit-test fixture (the only current caller of the hard_delete branch) to match.
2. Re-create routing (CORRECTED): do NOT route through `_apply_outbound_create` — that typed leaf
   (`apply_outbound.py:92-114`) has NO JQL dedup and NO `binding_store`/`bind_confirm`. Instead emit the
   follow-on as a typed `Mutation(direction=outbound, action=create, provenance={"origin":"hard_delete_recreate"})`
   so it flows through the EXISTING create dispatch (`dispatch_one.create_one`) that already does JQL dedup +
   `bind_confirm`. This needs NO new `MutationAction` enum member.
3. Follow-on wiring: `applier.py:469-471` currently only acts on `kind=="suppress_pair"`. Add a branch that
   collects `create_after_hard_delete` follow-ons into a pending-outbound list and injects them into the
   outbound batch before `_apply_batch` (specify the exact injection point in `apply()`/`reconcile_once`).
4. Idempotency: a pass dying after the Jira create but before binding persistence must NOT double-create on
   retry — `create_one`'s JQL dedup + `recover_pending_bindings` cover this; add a test.
5. Stale-binding lifecycle: after re-create, RETIRE the old (dead) binding that pointed at the hard-deleted
   issue so two bindings do not coexist and dedup ignores the dead one.
6. Guard: only genuine `hard_delete` triggers re-create (not redirect / out_of_window / trash).
7. Fix the misleading COMMENT regardless.
8. Tracking home: this epic (adept-hedge-stain) replaces the lost `3e36`; update the `TODO(epic-3e36)` comment
   at `apply_inbound.py:560` to point here and clear the `.joe-janitor/report-2026-07-02.md:137` flag.

## Scope
Reconciler inbound hard-delete -> outbound re-create. Edit targets: `apply_inbound.py`, `applier.py`,
`reconcile.py`, `dispatch_one.py` (via existing create path), tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Key mismatch fixed (canonical `reason`); hard_delete branch reachable in the real dispatch path; existing unit-test fixture updated.
- [ ] Re-create flows through `dispatch_one.create_one` (JQL dedup + `bind_confirm`) via a typed outbound `create` Mutation — no parallel create path, no new enum member.
- [ ] Follow-on consumer wiring added at `applier.py:469-471`; re-create injected into the outbound batch in the same pass.
- [ ] Idempotent: retry after partial failure does not double-create (test).
- [ ] Stale pre-delete binding retired after re-create; dedup ignores the dead issue.
- [ ] Only genuine hard-deletes re-create (redirect/out_of_window/trash do not).
- [ ] COMMENT accurately reflects what happened; `TODO(epic-3e36)` repointed to this epic; janitor flag cleared.
- [ ] End-to-end test: inbound hard-delete -> outbound re-create in the same pass.
